originalTitle: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
title: Friends from Far Away: A Story About People Moving in South Africa!
content:
Hello, little explorers and happy listeners! It's your friendly kid reporter from Africa here, ready to share a story with you. Today, we're going on a little adventure to a beautiful country called South Africa. It’s a very special place, full of sunshine, amazing animals, and friendly people!

Welcome, little explorers!

I’m so glad you’re here with me today. We're going to talk about people, and how sometimes people move from one home to another. It’s like when you move houses, or when your friend moves to a new town. It happens all the time, all over the world! Did you know? Africa is a super, duper big continent! It has many, many countries, just like a giant puzzle with lots of pieces. South Africa is one of those exciting pieces!

People Come and People Go

Imagine a little bird flying from one tree to another to find the best berries. People are sometimes like that bird! They might move from one country to another. Why do they do this, you ask? Well, sometimes people move to find a new job for their mom or dad. Sometimes they move to be closer to their family, like a grandma or grandpa. Or maybe they move because they want to feel safer, or just for an exciting new adventure! It’s a bit like going on a big trip, but for a longer time. Did you know? People have been moving and traveling for thousands of years! It's how people have shared ideas and made new friends across the world.

Meeting New Friends in South Africa

South Africa is a wonderful place, with huge mountains, sunny beaches, and big cities. Many people live there, and some of these people have come from other countries in Africa, or even from far, far away. They come to live, work, and build new lives. These new friends often bring their own special songs, yummy foods, and beautiful stories to share. This makes South Africa even more colourful and interesting! Did you know? South Africa has 11 official languages! That means people speak many different languages there, like Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, and English!

Special Papers, Special Rules

Now, imagine you want to go to a special club, like a soccer club or a dance club. Usually, you need a special card or a permission slip from your grown-ups, right? This card shows that you are allowed to be there. Well, when people move to a new country, they sometimes need special papers too. These papers are like a big permission slip from the government that says, 'You can live here and be safe.' Most people who move to South Africa have these special papers. But some people don't have these special papers. We call them 'undocumented.' It means they don't have the right permission from the government to live there. It’s not easy to get these papers, and sometimes people arrive without them. Did you know? Having these special papers helps countries know who is living there, so they can plan for schools, hospitals, and jobs for everyone.

Some People Are Unhappy

In South Africa, there are some people who live there all the time. They are called 'residents.' Some of these residents are feeling a bit unhappy. They are worried. They are worried that there might not be enough jobs for everyone. Or maybe they think there are not enough houses for everyone to live in. When people feel worried or unhappy about something, they sometimes gather together. These groups are called 'protesters.' Did you know? When people protest, they are trying to show everyone that something is very important to them and they want a change to happen.

A Special Date: June 30th

These people who are unhappy have set a special date. It's the 30th of June. They have said that they would like all the people who don't have special papers to go back to their own countries by this date. This is a very big ask, and it makes many people feel sad or scared. It’s a bit like saying, 'Everyone who doesn't have a red shirt has to leave the party by dinner time!' It’s a tricky situation, and it can be hard for everyone involved. Did you know? Dates like June 30th are called 'deadlines.' They help us know when something important needs to happen, like when your homework is due!

What Happens Now?

So, what happens next? This is a big problem that needs grown-ups and leaders to talk and think very carefully. The leaders of South Africa and other countries are working hard. They want to find a kind and fair way to solve this problem. They are talking about how everyone can live safely and happily. They are trying to make sure there are enough schools, homes, and jobs for everyone, no matter where they come from. It takes a lot of listening and talking to find the best answers. Did you know? Good leaders listen to everyone and try their best to make things fair for all people in their country!

Being Kind to Everyone

No matter where people come from, everyone deserves to be treated with kindness. Imagine if you moved to a new place. You would want new friends to be nice to you, wouldn't you? We all want to feel safe and have a good home. It’s important to remember that everyone has feelings, and everyone wants to be happy. Even if people are worried, being kind and understanding is always the best way to make things better. Did you know? When we are kind to others, it makes us feel good inside too! Kindness is like a superpower that makes the world a better place.

A Bright Future for Africa

Africa is a continent full of love, hope, and amazing people. We can all learn to live together, share our beautiful land, and help each other. When we work together and are kind to each other, our future will shine super brightly! Let’s all wish for peace and happiness for everyone in South Africa and all over our wonderful continent. Remember to always be kind, gentle, and understanding to everyone you meet. Did you know? Africa is home to some of the most beautiful wildlife in the world, like lions, elephants, giraffes, and zebras! They all live together, too! That's all for now from your friendly reporter. Keep exploring and keep being kind!
summary: Some people in South Africa want people who do not have special papers to live there to go back to their home countries by June 30th because they are worried about jobs and homes.
whyMatters: This is important because it's about how people from different places live together in a country, and it reminds us that everyone deserves to be treated with kindness and understanding, no matter where they come from.

originalTitle: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
title: Searching for Little Kofi: Why Hospitals Are Our Friends!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter, here from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, I want to talk about something important that happened in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a big, beautiful country, just like many others in Africa. Imagine a little boy, let's call him Kofi. He is only six years old, just like some of you! Kofi was feeling very, very sick. He had a sickness called Ebola. Now, Ebola is a serious sickness, but guess what? Doctors know how to help people get better from it! So, Kofi was in a hospital, a special place where doctors and nurses take care of sick people. They were helping him get strong again. But then, something sad happened. Some grown-ups, who were very scared and confused, went to the hospital. They took Little Kofi away. This made everyone very, very worried. The doctors and nurses were sad because they couldn't help Kofi anymore. And they worried about Kofi because he needs special care to get better. This is why many good people are now looking for Kofi, so he can get the help he needs.

What is Ebola Sickness?

Now, some of you might have heard of Ebola. It sounds a bit scary, right? But don't worry, I'm here to tell you about it simply. Ebola is a sickness, like when you get a bad cold or a tummy ache, but much, much more serious. It makes people feel very weak, with a fever and a sore body. Did you know? Ebola is not like a cold or cough that spreads through the air when someone sneezes. No! It only spreads when you touch someone who is very sick with Ebola, or touch their blood or other body fluids. That's why doctors wear special, full-body suits to keep themselves safe while they help. Doctors and nurses are super smart! They know how to treat Ebola. They give special medicines and help the body fight the sickness. Their main job is to help sick people get better and also make sure the sickness doesn't spread to other people. That's why hospitals are so important for these kinds of sicknesses.

Why Hospitals Are Our Super Safe Places

Hospitals are not scary places, my friends. They are like giant superhero headquarters! Inside, you find amazing heroes: doctors, nurses, and many other helpers. Their superpower is helping people feel better and stay healthy. When you are sick, where do you go? To the doctor, right? Doctors in hospitals have special machines and medicines that can help us. They have clean beds, yummy food to make you strong, and friendly faces to cheer you up. Hospitals are designed to be very clean so that germs don't spread. Did you know? In many hospitals across Africa, doctors and nurses work incredibly hard, sometimes even far from their own families, just to help people in their communities. They are true heroes! They learn for many, many years to know how to fix broken bones, give medicine for fevers, and even help tiny babies be born healthy.

Why Were People Scared?

So, why did those grown-ups take Little Kofi from the hospital? It's because they were very scared and they didn't have the right information. Sometimes, when people don't understand something, they get very, very afraid. This fear can make them believe things that are not true. Some people might hear wrong stories, like "doctors make people sicker" or "hospitals are where you catch more diseases." But these are just rumors! Rumors are like whispers that fly around, but they aren't true. Doctors are trying their best to save lives. They want to help, not harm. Did you know? It's super important to always ask a trusted grown-up, like your parent, teacher, or doctor, if you hear something that sounds scary or confusing. They can help you find out the truth! Learning the truth helps us not be so scared.

The Big Problem with Attacking Hospitals

When grown-ups get scared and attack a hospital, it causes a lot of trouble. First, it hurts the doctors and nurses. They are trying to help, and it's not fair to scare them. Second, it stops other sick people from getting the help they need. Imagine if your friend had a very bad cut, but they couldn't go to the hospital because it wasn't safe. That would be very sad! Little Kofi was getting special care for Ebola. When he was taken away, it meant he stopped getting his medicine and special food. It's like trying to finish a puzzle but someone keeps taking pieces away! He needs those pieces – the doctors' help – to get well. Did you know? Keeping hospitals safe is everyone's job! When we protect hospitals, we are protecting ourselves and our communities because everyone might need a hospital one day.

What Can We Do to Help?

Even though you are kids, you are very powerful! You can help in many ways. 1. **Listen to Trusted Grown-ups**: Always listen to your parents, teachers, and doctors. They have important knowledge to share. 2. **Share True Information**: If you hear scary rumors, remember what I told you. Tell your friends that hospitals are safe and doctors are helpers. Don't spread false stories. 3. **Be Kind and Understanding**: Try to understand why people might be scared. Maybe they don't know the truth. You can help by sharing simple, correct information. 4. **Be Hopeful**: Let's all hope and maybe even say a little prayer for Little Kofi to be found soon and for him to get the special care he needs to get strong again. Our doctors and nurses in Africa and all over the world are doing amazing work. They are heroes in scrubs! We need to protect them and their hospitals so they can keep helping everyone, especially little children like Kofi, get healthy and strong.

Let's Be Heroes Too!

So, my young friends, remember this: doctors are our friends, hospitals are our safe places, and learning the truth helps us be brave. When we work together and trust our health heroes, we can fight sickness and build a healthier, happier Africa for everyone. Let's send positive thoughts to Little Kofi and all the brave doctors and nurses working hard every day! Thank you for listening, and stay safe and smart!
summary: A six-year-old boy named Kofi, sick with Ebola, was taken from a hospital in DR Congo by scared grown-ups, and now doctors are looking for him to give him the special care he needs to get well.
whyMatters: This is important because hospitals are safe places where doctors help sick people get better, and everyone needs to understand the truth about sicknesses like Ebola so that doctors can do their vital work without fear and save lives.

originalTitle: Nigerian man jailed for storing human faeces outside his home
title: Clean Homes, Happy Hearts! A Story from Nigeria
content:
Welcome, Little Reporters!

Hello, my super curious friends! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter, here to tell you a story from a place not too far away, right here in our wonderful Africa. We are going to learn about why it's super important to keep our homes and the places around us very, very clean. Are you ready for an adventure in learning?

What Happened in Nigeria?

Once upon a time, not so long ago, in a bustling town in Nigeria, there was a man. He lived in a house, just like many of us do. But something went wrong. This man kept some very smelly things right outside his home. These things were not good. They were human waste, like what goes into a toilet. Can you imagine? His neighbours, the people who lived next door and all around him, started to notice a very strong, bad smell. This smell was so yucky that it made them feel unwell. It made them sad, and they couldn't even play or eat or sleep comfortably in their own houses because of the terrible smell. Imagine trying to play your favourite game and all you can smell is something awful! It's no fun at all.

What is a "Bad Smell"?

A bad smell is something that makes your nose scrunch up! It's not like the lovely smell of jollof rice cooking, or fresh flowers, or clean clothes. No, this was a very, very strong and unpleasant smell, like rotten food or rubbish that has been left for too long. When a smell is this bad, we can call it a 'stench'. *Did you know?* Our noses are amazing! They help us to smell yummy food, but they also warn us when something is not good, like when food has gone bad or when something is dirty. So, our nose is like a little helper!

Why Are Bad Smells Bad?

Bad smells are not just annoying; they can be very dangerous for our health. When things like human waste are left outside, they carry tiny, tiny invisible things called germs. These germs are so small, you cannot see them without a special microscope! But these germs can make people very, very sick. If you breathe in air with lots of these germs, or if germs get on your hands and then you touch your mouth, you can get a tummy ache, or a fever, or other sicknesses. The neighbours in Nigeria were worried because they knew this bad smell could make them and their children ill. Nobody wants to be sick, right? *Did you know?* Germs are everywhere, but many of them are good! However, some germs can be naughty and cause sickness. That's why we wash our hands often!

Keeping Our Homes Clean

This story teaches us a very important lesson: keeping our homes and the areas around them clean is super, super important. When we keep things tidy, we stop bad smells from forming. We also chase away those naughty germs. A clean home is a happy home, and a healthy home! It's not just about inside your house. It's also about what's right outside your door, where you play and where your neighbours live. Everyone deserves to live in a place that smells nice and is safe. *Did you know?* In many African cultures, cleanliness is a very big deal! People take great pride in making sure their homes and compounds are neat and welcoming.

Using Toilets the Right Way

So, what do we do with human waste? We always, always use the toilet! Toilets are special places designed to take waste far away so it doesn't hurt anyone or make things smell bad. After using the toilet, we always flush it, and then we wash our hands with soap and clean water. This is the very best way to keep germs away. When waste is left outside, it pollutes the air. It can also get into the soil and even into the water we drink. This can make many, many people sick. So, using toilets properly is a big step in keeping our whole community healthy. *Did you know?* Many places in Africa are working hard to make sure everyone has a clean and safe toilet to use. It's called 'sanitation', and it's super important!

Rules for a Happy Community

Every town and village has rules. These rules are like friendly guides that help everyone live together nicely and safely. One of these rules is about keeping our environment clean. When the man in Nigeria kept the smelly things outside his house, he broke an important rule. These rules are there to protect everyone, especially children like you. When someone breaks a rule that affects many people's health and happiness, sometimes there are consequences. Like in our story, the man faced a problem because his actions made many people unhappy and worried about getting sick. It's a reminder that rules are there to help us all be happy and healthy together. *Did you know?* Just like your school has rules to help everyone learn and play safely, towns and cities have rules to help everyone live well together!

Being a Good Neighbour

Imagine your best friend lives next door. You want to be a good friend, right? Being a good neighbour is just like that! It means thinking about the people around you. It means making sure your actions don't make your neighbours sad, or sick, or uncomfortable. When we are good neighbours, we help create a happy and strong community. If we all do our part, keeping our own little space clean and tidy, then the whole community becomes clean and tidy. This makes everyone feel safer, happier, and proud of where they live. It means more fun time and less worrying about bad smells or germs! *Did you know?* In many African communities, neighbours are like family. They help each other, share things, and look out for one another.

How We Can All Help

So, what can YOU do, my little reporters? You can do so much! You can always use the toilet and flush it. You can wash your hands with soap and water many times a day, especially after playing and before eating. You can help throw rubbish in the bin, not on the ground. You can even tell your grown-ups if you see something that looks dirty or smells bad. Every little bit helps! When we all work together, we can make our homes, our villages, and our towns super clean, super safe, and super happy places for everyone to live and play. *Did you know?* Washing your hands properly for 20 seconds is like singing 'Happy Birthday' twice! It helps chase away almost all the bad germs.

Let's Stay Healthy and Happy!

Remember, a clean environment means healthy bodies and happy hearts. Let's all be champions of cleanliness, just like amazing kids all over Africa! Keep exploring, keep learning, and keep helping to make our world a better, cleaner place. See you next time, super kids!
summary: A man in Nigeria faced a problem because he kept very smelly human waste outside his house, making his neighbours sick and unhappy, which showed how important it is to keep our homes and surroundings clean and follow community rules.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that keeping our homes and the areas outside clean is super important for everyone's health and happiness, preventing bad smells and germs from spreading, and helping us all live together nicely in our communities.

originalTitle: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
title: A Super Special Park: Learning About Sharing in Jerusalem!
content:
Jambo, my amazing friends in Africa! It's me, your friendly news reporter, ready to tell you about something important happening far, far away, in a city called Jerusalem. It’s a story about sharing, rules, and being kind to everyone, just like we learn in our communities right here in Africa!

Hello from Jerusalem!

Imagine a super, super old city. This city is called Jerusalem. It's a special place that many people from all over the world love. It's so old that maybe even your great-great-great-grandparents weren't born when this city was first built! It has tiny, winding streets and amazing buildings that tell stories of many, many years ago. It’s a place filled with history and wonder.

A Very Special Park

In the middle of this old city, there is a very big and very beautiful park. It's not just any park, my dears! This park has ancient, old buildings, tall walls, and even some shining, golden domes that glitter in the sun. It’s like a magical place from a storybook. People call this big park the "Al-Aqsa Mosque Compound." It's a very big space with different parts, and each part is important in its own way. Think of it like a community center with different rooms for different activities, but much, much older and holier!

Why Is It So Special?

Now, why is this park so important? Well, it's special to two big groups of people who have different ways of praying to God. Imagine if two of your best friends both had a favorite spot in your school playground. This park is like that, but for whole groups of people who have very strong beliefs. For our Muslim friends around the world, this big park holds a very holy building called the Al-Aqsa Mosque. It is one of the most important places in the world for Muslims to pray to God. They believe it's a place where they can feel very close to God. It is so important that people travel from far away just to visit and pray here. Did you know? Muslims pray five times a day, facing towards a very special place called the Kaaba in Mecca! But places like the Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem are also super holy and cherished by Muslims everywhere. But guess what? For our Jewish friends, this very same park is also super, super special! They call it the "Temple Mount." A long, long, long time ago, their very first and most important holy temples stood right here. These temples were amazing places where Jewish people would pray to God. They also pray to God, but in their own special ways, and often near the outside walls of this big park, at a place called the Western Wall. It’s a place that holds many memories and prayers for them. Did you know? Jerusalem is often called the "Holy City" because it is very important to three big religions: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam! How amazing is it that one city can hold so much meaning for so many people?

Sharing is Caring: The Rules

Because this amazing park is so special to two different groups of people, there have been very important rules about how everyone shares it. Imagine if you have a favorite toy, and you need to share it with your friend. You both make rules so you can play nicely and fairly, right? Like, "You play for five minutes, then I play for five minutes." These rules are like an old, old agreement that all the grown-ups made a very long time ago. They help everyone feel safe and respected when they visit this special place. For a very long time, these rules have said that Muslims can pray inside the Al-Aqsa Mosque buildings whenever they want. And Jewish people can visit the big park area, walk around, and enjoy the beauty, but they usually pray in a special spot outside the main mosque buildings, near the Western Wall. This way, everyone has their special place, and everyone knows what to do, making sure there is peace.

When Rules Are Not Followed

But sometimes, grown-ups don't always follow the rules. It's like when you and your friend agree to take turns on the swing, but then someone keeps swinging for too long, or tries to use the slide in a different way than you both agreed! That makes the other person feel sad or worried, right? It can cause arguments and stop the fun. Some grown-ups, who are Israeli, love their country very much. They are sometimes called "nationalists." They feel very strongly about their beliefs and want to show how important this place is to them. Sometimes, these people go into the special park and try to pray inside the buildings, or do things that are against the old sharing rules that everyone agreed upon. They might feel like the rules should be different. When this happens, it makes our Muslim friends feel very, very worried and upset. They feel like their special prayer place is not being respected. This can cause big problems and make people feel angry instead of friends. It can feel like someone is not listening to the old agreement, and that can make the peace in the park feel shaky. Did you know? When people talk and listen to each other with open hearts and minds, it's much easier to solve problems and keep the peace. Listening is a superpower!

Why Rules Keep Us Safe and Happy

Following rules is super important, my dears. Rules help everyone feel safe and happy. They help us share, understand each other, and live in peace. When everyone respects the rules, it means we respect each other's feelings and special places. It shows that we care about what is important to others, even if it's different from what's important to us. Think about your classroom. If everyone follows the rules, like raising their hand to speak or sharing crayons, then everyone has a good time and learns a lot. No one feels left out or upset. It’s the same in this very special park in Jerusalem. When everyone follows the old sharing rules, the park can be a calm and happy place for everyone who visits.

Let's Share and Be Friends!

So, what can we learn from this story happening far away? We learn that sharing is important. Respecting rules is important. And understanding that different people have different special places and different ways of praying is also very important. We can all try to be kind, listen to each other, and remember that peace is the best way forward for everyone, no matter where they live or what they believe. Did you know? Many schools and communities in Africa teach about being kind to everyone, no matter where they come from or what they believe! We know that when we are kind and understanding, we build a happier world. Let’s hope that everyone in Jerusalem remembers the importance of sharing and following the rules, so this beautiful, special park can be a place of peace for everyone, always. Let’s all practice sharing and kindness in our own homes and playgrounds too! Bye for now, from your friend in Africa!
summary: Some people are not following the old rules about how different religions share a very special park in Jerusalem, which makes others worried.
whyMatters: This is important because when people don't follow sharing rules in special places, it can cause arguments and make people sad instead of being friends and living in peace.
